_notes: Twinings incidents — mapped from 5 incident categories in workbook. MTTR in hours (not minutes) — this is the core ZeroOps story. IDs use TWNG format as assumed from ServiceNow.

incidents.svc: SAP S/4HANA — Master Data Governance Queue Critical
incidents.notes: MDG approval queue at 847 items. GL/COA master data corrections blocked. Finance period-close at risk. K2 approval workflows timing out waiting for MDG responses. ZeroOps RCA Engine identifying batch scheduling conflict in dialog WP class.
incidents.desc: SAP Master Data Governance approval queue has reached 847 pending items — the highest level recorded this quarter. Finance master data corrections including GL account extensions and COA updates are blocked, directly impacting period-end close activities. K2 procurement approval workflows are timing out as they wait for MDG responses that are 299+ hours in the queue. 1,806 users experiencing downstream impacts across S/4HANA, Ariba, and OpenText.

intel.rootCause: SAP MDG approval queue critically overloaded — 847 items with zero automation. All master data corrections requiring manual reviewer assignment from a pool of 12 approvers. Batch MDG processing jobs scheduled in dialog work process class competing with user sessions during business hours.
causalChain: MDG batch processing jobs scheduled in dialog WP class — competing with 1,806 user sessions
Dialog WP pool at 74% — approver sessions experiencing latency
Queue ingestion rate (125/month) exceeding manual processing capacity (approx 80/month)
847 items in queue — average age 299 hours — 85% SLA breach rate
Downstream: Ariba PO processing blocked · K2 workflows timing out · Finance close delayed
intel.recommendation: IMMEDIATE: Move MDG batch jobs to background WP class via SM37 — 15min to implement. ZeroOps: auto-validate standard master data corrections (field mappings, pricing) — estimated 60% of queue eligible. MEDIUM-TERM: MDG workflow automation for standard corrections. PERMANENT: Auto-escalation when queue exceeds 200 items.

incidents.svc: SAP Analytics Cloud — Month-End Report Generation Timeout
incidents.notes: SAC report generation averaging 8.4 minutes vs 2-minute SLA. BW/4HANA live connection latency 4.2 seconds. 47 concurrent finance users during month-end peak. RCA Engine identifying CDS view non-optimisation as root cause.
incidents.desc: SAP Analytics Cloud report generation has degraded significantly during month-end reporting peak. Finance and management reporting teams are experiencing 8.4-minute average report generation times against a 2-minute SLA target. The BW/4HANA live data connection is showing 4.2-second latency due to suboptimal CDS view queries triggering full table scans on FI-GL aggregates. 47 concurrent users are affected during the critical month-end close window.

intel.rootCause: SAC live connection to BW/4HANA triggering full table scans on FI-GL aggregates — CDS views not configured for HANA query optimisation. Month-end peak (47 concurrent users) saturating BW query engine.
causalChain: BW/4HANA CDS views without HANA-optimised query push-down
SAC live connection executing full table scans — avg 8.4 min per report
Month-end: 47 concurrent report users — BW HANA memory at 71%
Priority queue not configured — month-end executive reports wait same as ad-hoc queries
272-hour average MTTR — reports simply join the incident queue
intel.recommendation: ZeroOps RCA Engine generates HANA calculation view recommendation for the top 5 CDS views. Implement SAC report priority classes — P1: executive dashboards, P2: management reports, P3: ad-hoc. ZeroOps Capacity Planner defers ad-hoc queries during month-end peak automatically.

incidents.svc: SAP IAS — Authorization Request Backlog: 80 Open Requests
incidents.notes: 80 open authorization requests. New starters waiting 11+ days for SAP system access. SoD checks being performed manually. Fiori catalog assignments requiring BASIS admin. Average resolution time 281 hours.
incidents.desc: SAP Identity and Access Management is experiencing a critical authorization request backlog with 80 open items averaging 281 hours resolution time. New employee onboarding is severely impacted — staff are waiting over 11 days for SAP system access before they can be productive. The current manual SoD checking process and BASIS admin involvement for every Fiori catalog assignment is unsustainable at 300 authorization requests per month volume.

intel.rootCause: SAP IAS authorization workflow entirely manual — no automated SoD checking, no standard role auto-approval, no Fiori catalog auto-assignment. 300 monthly requests processed by manual review team of 12 with no prioritisation.
causalChain: 300 authorization requests/month — manual review only
SoD check manual — average 2-3 hours per complex request
Fiori catalog assignment requires BASIS admin availability — creates additional queue
New starters: 11+ day average wait for system access
80 open requests at 281-hour average — compliance risk accumulating
intel.recommendation: ZeroOps Change Validator can auto-approve standard Fiori catalog assignments (read-only access, display roles) after automated SoD check — estimated 70% of queue eligible. Human approval retained for sensitive transaction access. Target: 300 requests/month → <4hr resolution for standard, same-day for emergency.

incidents.svc: Ariba — Procurement Blocked: Supplier Master Data Pending
incidents.notes: 23 purchase orders blocked on Ariba — supplier master data not yet approved in S/4HANA MDG. iFlow integration retrying every 30 minutes. Procurement team tracking manually via email. Directly linked to INC-TWNG-00447 MDG backlog.
incidents.desc: 23 purchase orders on Ariba are blocked pending supplier master data approval in SAP MDG. The S/4HANA to Ariba master data synchronisation iFlow is retrying every 30 minutes but cannot proceed until the upstream MDG approval queue releases the relevant supplier records. The procurement team is manually tracking affected POs via email threads. This incident is a downstream consequence of the MDG backlog in INC-TWNG-00447.

intel.rootCause: Ariba procurement workflow blocked by upstream SAP MDG supplier master data approval backlog. Ariba-S/4HANA integration iFlow cannot synchronise unapproved supplier records — 23 POs on hold pending master data release.
causalChain: Supplier master data corrections submitted to MDG — 6 records pending approval
MDG queue: estimated 299 hours wait time for these records
Ariba iFlow polling every 30 minutes — all attempts returning pending status
23 POs cannot be issued without approved supplier master data
Procurement team manually tracking — 4 hours of manual effort already spent
intel.recommendation: ZeroOps can priority-flag MDG items with downstream Ariba PO blocking — auto-escalate to senior approver bypassing standard queue. Estimated resolution: <2 hours for priority items. Long-term: MDG-Ariba dependency monitoring with auto-escalation trigger.

incidents.notes: K2 work-instruction workflows timing out after 24 hours while waiting for upstream S/4HANA approvals that are averaging 224 hours. 61 workflow instances requiring manual restart. ServiceNow catalog items backing up.
incidents.desc: K2 Cloud workflow approval sessions are timing out at the 24-hour threshold while awaiting upstream S/4HANA approval responses that average 224 hours. 61 work-instruction workflow instances have expired and require manual restart by the service desk team. The ServiceNow service catalogue items associated with these workflows are showing as stalled, causing further confusion for end users who submitted requests expecting timely fulfillment.

intel.rootCause: K2 workflow session timeout (24 hours) incompatible with S/4HANA approval response times (224+ hours average). Structural mismatch between K2 timeout configuration and actual upstream processing times.
causalChain: K2 workflow timeout: 24 hours
S/4HANA work-instruction approval average: 224 hours
K2 sessions expire before approvals return — auto-cancel triggered
61 instances requiring manual service desk restart
ServiceNow catalog items showing stalled — users raising duplicate requests
intel.recommendation: ZeroOps can detect K2 timeout patterns and auto-restart workflows once upstream approval completes — no manual service desk intervention. Alternatively, auto-escalate upstream S/4HANA work-instruction items with pending K2 dependency. Estimated: eliminate 61 manual restarts/month.

incidents.svc: SAP Fiori — L2 Session Reset Required: Batch of 14 Users
incidents.notes: 14 users experiencing Fiori tile loading errors after overnight system maintenance. ZeroOps Alert Correlator detected the pattern — cache invalidation event during maintenance window. Automated cache flush and session reset executed. All 14 users operational before business hours.
incidents.desc: 14 SAP Fiori users reported tile loading errors following the overnight system maintenance window. The ZeroOps Alert Correlator detected the pattern within 47 seconds of the first alert — correlating all 14 affected user sessions to a cache invalidation event that occurred at 03:44 during the maintenance window. ZeroOps executed an automated cache flush and user session reset. All 14 users were operational before 04:00 — before the business day started. No service desk call was required.

intel.rootCause: SAP Fiori tile cache invalidated during overnight maintenance window at 03:44 — cache not automatically refreshed post-maintenance. 14 users who had persistent sessions affected.
causalChain: Overnight maintenance window: SAP PCE patch applied at 03:30
Fiori tile cache invalidated as part of patch process
Post-maintenance cache refresh not included in maintenance runbook
14 users with persistent sessions encountering stale cache on 04:00 login
ZeroOps detected pattern: 47 seconds · correlated to maintenance event
intel.recommendation: Runbook updated: Fiori cache flush now included in post-maintenance checklist and automated by ZeroOps. Pattern stored in vector DB — future occurrences auto-resolved without human involvement.

incidents.svc: SAP BW/4HANA — DTP Chain Failure: Inventory Data Load
incidents.notes: BW/4HANA nightly DTP chain for inventory data failed at step 3 of 7. Delta extraction from S/4HANA inventory management failed due to open change pointer from incomplete goods movement posting. ZeroOps RCA Engine identified the stale change pointer. Remediation Agent executed RSSM change pointer reset. DTP chain restarted successfully.
incidents.desc: The SAP BW/4HANA nightly data transfer process chain for inventory data failed at step 3 of 7 at 22:15. ZeroOps RCA Engine identified the root cause as a stale delta change pointer in S/4HANA inventory management — left open by an incomplete goods movement posting from earlier in the day. The Remediation Agent executed the RSSM change pointer reset runbook and restarted the DTP chain. The inventory data load completed successfully by 00:29, within the nightly load window.

intel.rootCause: BW/4HANA DTP chain failure — stale delta change pointer in S/4HANA inventory management from incomplete goods movement posting at 18:42. Change pointer not cleaned up automatically.
causalChain: Goods movement posting INC incomplete at 18:42 — session timeout during posting
S/4HANA delta change pointer created but not closed
BW/4HANA nightly DTP chain at 22:15 — delta extraction fails on open pointer
Chain fails at step 3 — inventory data for next day morning reporting unavailable
intel.recommendation: Resolved. ZeroOps pattern stored — future delta pointer failures will be auto-detected and reset. RSSM change pointer cleanup now runs pre-DTP chain as automated health check.

incidents.svc: SAP S/4HANA — Period-End Close Blocked: Background Jobs Failed
incidents.notes: Period-end close background jobs (FI_POST_CLOSE, GL_RECON) failed at month start. Root cause: SM37 background job log full — 99% utilisation preventing new job scheduling. ZeroOps executed SM37 log purge and rescheduled failed jobs. Finance close completed on time.
incidents.desc: SAP S/4HANA period-end close background jobs FI_POST_CLOSE and GL_RECON failed to start at month-beginning scheduled time. ZeroOps RCA Engine identified the root cause within 4 minutes — the SM37 background job log had reached 99% utilisation, preventing new jobs from scheduling. The Remediation Agent executed the SM37 log purge runbook and rescheduled the failed jobs. Finance period-end close completed on time. Without ZeroOps, the estimated manual resolution time was 4+ hours.

intel.rootCause: SAP SM37 background job log at 99% utilisation — insufficient log space preventing period-end close job scheduling. Log retention policy not set, accumulating 18 months of job logs.
causalChain: SM37 background job log: no retention policy — accumulating since go-live
Log reached 99% at 06:09 — new job scheduling blocked
Period-end close jobs FI_POST_CLOSE + GL_RECON failed to start at 06:15
Finance team discovered at 07:30 — period-end close window at risk
intel.recommendation: Resolved. SM37 log retention policy set to 90 days and automated. ZeroOps monitors SM37 log utilisation — alert at 70%, auto-purge at 80%. Pattern now in vector DB for auto-resolution.

incidents.svc: SAP Concur — Expense Integration Sync Delayed
incidents.notes: Concur-S/4HANA expense data synchronisation delayed — integration service account session expired. ZeroOps detected authentication failure pattern. Change Validator renewed service account session automatically. 847 expense reports synced. No service desk involvement.
incidents.desc: SAP Concur expense data synchronisation to S/4HANA experienced a 4-hour delay when the integration service account session expired without renewal. ZeroOps Alert Correlator detected the authentication failure pattern within 6 minutes and correlated it to the session expiry event. The Change Validator automatically renewed the service account session and triggered a catch-up sync of 847 pending expense reports. All expense data was synchronised and available in S/4HANA before end of business. No service desk involvement was required.

intel.rootCause: Concur-S/4HANA integration service account session expired — no automated renewal configured. 847 expense reports queued pending sync.
causalChain: Service account session expiry at 10:18 — renewal not automated
Concur sync job failing silently — no alert configured on auth failure
847 expense reports accumulating in sync queue
Finance team discovers at 14:15 — expense data unavailable for posting
intel.recommendation: Resolved. Service account auto-renewal now managed by ZeroOps Change Validator. Session expiry monitoring added — alert at 24hrs before expiry. Pattern in vector DB for future auto-resolution.

incidents.svc: Snowflake — Pipeline Latency: SAP Master Data Feed
incidents.notes: Snowflake data warehouse latency elevated — Talend pipeline from SAP ingesting MDG master data corrections that were invalid due to incomplete approval. ZeroOps Capacity Planner identified the invalid record pattern and quarantined affected records. Pipeline throughput restored. Data quality alert raised for manual MDG review.
incidents.desc: Snowflake data warehouse pipeline latency increased when the Talend ETL pipeline began ingesting SAP master data records that had been partially approved in MDG — creating inconsistent data in the Snowflake analytics layer. ZeroOps Capacity Planner identified the invalid record pattern within 3 minutes, automatically quarantined 47 affected records, and restored normal pipeline throughput. A data quality alert was raised for the MDG team to complete the pending approvals and trigger a data correction load.

intel.rootCause: Talend pipeline ingesting partially-approved SAP MDG master data records — inconsistent data state created by MDG approval backlog causing records to be partially updated.
causalChain: MDG approval backlog releasing partial records to downstream systems
Talend pipeline ingesting partial records into Snowflake
Snowflake data quality checks failing — pipeline latency increasing
Analytics dashboards showing inconsistent master data
intel.recommendation: Resolved. ZeroOps now monitors MDG record completeness before Talend pipeline ingestion — incomplete records quarantined automatically. Root fix: MDG approval backlog reduction (see INC-TWNG-00447).
